findings.evidence: Existing API has `unweightedAbelJacobiClass` at `AbelJacobi.lean:99` and unweighted splitting lemmas at `AbelJacobiDegreeSplitting.lean:109`, but `rg weightedAbelJacobiDivisorClass|unweightedAbelJacobiDivisorClass` only finds weighted declarations in this new file.
findings.fix: Add `unweightedAbelJacobiDivisorClass` as the constant-weight specialization, with coe/ofPoint/base/sum/principal lemmas paralleling the weighted API.
findings.issue: Only the weighted divisor-sum map is exposed, while the surrounding Abel-Jacobi API exposes unweighted specializations for the same constructions.

findings.evidence: Point-level API provides `weightedAbelJacobiClass_eq_iff_divisorClass` and `_eq_iff_linearlyEquivalent` in `AbelJacobi.lean:82` and `AbelJacobi.lean:91`; the new file only has `weightedAbelJacobiDivisorClass_eq_of_linearlyEquivalent_of_weightedDegree_eq` at line 145.
findings.fix: Add iff lemmas reducing equality to equality of the corrected divisor classes and to linear equivalence of `D - weightedDegree w D • ofPoint x₀` and `E - weightedDegree w E • ofPoint x₀`.
findings.issue: The API gives only one sufficient invariance lemma, not the elimination/iff lemmas characterizing equality of two divisor Abel-Jacobi sums.

findings.evidence: Existing point API has `classGroupAddEquivPicZeroProdInt_divisorClass_ofPoint` at `AbelJacobiDegreeSplitting.lean:63`; the new file has only the coerced class-group formula at line 76 and the finite-sum formula at line 122.
findings.fix: Add a simp lemma `S.classGroupAddEquivPicZeroProdInt w h hx₀ (S.divisorClass D) = (S.weightedAbelJacobiDivisorClass w h hx₀ D, weightedDegree w D)` and the corresponding inverse reconstruction lemma if needed downstream.
findings.issue: There is no characteristic lemma for how `S.divisorClass D` decomposes under `classGroupAddEquivPicZeroProdInt` using the new divisor Abel-Jacobi sum.

summary: The new homomorphism is minimal and its body is not exposed, but the public API is missing characteristic forms needed to use it with the existing Abel-Jacobi/splitting interface without rebuilding proofs by hand.
